title: Margo Flarestream

{{Quote|It saddens me when one of our warriors has to be removed from the fight for any reason. It saddens me even more when a woman of Pepper's capability has to remove herself because of guilt and pain. Fortunately, she can continue to contribute in her own way and, perhaps, she will be ready for front-line action again someday. Now, she is performing invaluable service anyway—even if she doesn't recognize that fact.|General Airen Cracken|<ref name="CRO" />}}
'''Margo "Pepper" Flarestream''', a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] around [[35 BBY/Legends|35 BBY]], defected from the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]] to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] in the early [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. A skilled [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], Flarestream rose to the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Flight Commander/Legends|Flight Commander]], flying [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]] and earning the nickname "Pepper" at some point. Flarestream led the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] forces during the [[Last Stand at Refnar|evacuation]] of the [[Unidentified Alliance base (Refnar)|Alliance base]] at [[Refnar]]. Although the [[Space warfare/Legends|engagement]] at Refnar was technically a victory for the Alliance—it evacuated almost 70 percent of all forces—Flarestream became guilt-ridden after her actions led to the destruction of many [[Transport/Legends|transports]] at the hands of an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Unidentified Imperial cruiser (Refnar)|cruiser]].

Haunted by her actions, Flarestream requested a transfer away from combat duty and was assigned to [[Sleeper Cell V-16]], based in the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Fabrillan]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Malthor]]. Although she descended into [[Alcohol/Legends|alcoholism]], Flarestream was a capable member of the cell. She knew many of the other pilots who frequented Fabrillian's [[Cantina/Legends|cantinas]], and she used her personal [[Ghtroc Industries/Legends|Ghtroc Industries]] [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]], the ''[[Pepper's Hope]]'', to extract Alliance [[operative]]s on the planet when the need arose. Despite Flarestream's emotional state, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]], head of [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]], was pleased that an individual of her caliber was still able to contribute to the Alliance, and he hoped that she would return to combat duty one day.

==Biography==
Margo "Pepper" Flarestream, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]],<ref name="CRO" /> was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] around the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[35 BBY/Legends|35 BBY]].<ref name="Birth" /> When she was old enough, she enrolled in the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]], but she defected to the fledgling [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the early [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Enlisting in the [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]], Flarestream qualified to fly [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]]. Acquiring the nickname "Pepper," Flarestream fought in several [[Starfighter combat/Legends|battles]] and eventually rose to the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Flight Commander/Legends|Flight Commander]]. At some point prior to the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces discovered an [[Unidentified Alliance base (Refnar)|Alliance base]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Refnar]],<ref name="CRO" /> in the [[Pakuuni sector/Legends|Pakuuni sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA-OC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> An [[Last Stand at Refnar|evacuation]] was ordered, and Flarestream was placed in [[Commander/Legends|command]] of the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] charged with defending the fleeing [[Transport/Legends|transports]] and [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]]. It was Flarestream's first [[Time/Legends|time]] in command.<ref name="CRO" />

The plan was simple and familiar; Flarestream and her [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] were to [[Space warfare/Legends|engage]] and delay the Imperial force—which consisted of a line [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] and [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]] support—long enough for the freighters and [[Troop transport/Legends|troop transports]] to escape into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. The starfighters were not expected to defeat the Imperials, but to survive long enough to accomplish the mission and withdraw. Standard tactics were to deploy in a picket line, forcing the enemy fighters to pass through their formation to attack the fleeing [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. However, Flarestream had other ideas. The TIE/LN starfighter had become the standard Imperial fighter, and since its deployment, average Alliance losses during a retreat had risen sharply. Alliance tacticians expected to [[Death/Legends|lose]] between 60 to 70 percent of their starfighter forces and 30 percent of their transports during a withdrawal. Faced with that information, Flarestream decided that she did not like those kind of odds.<ref name="CRO" />

Flarestream deployed her starfighters in an [[over-under formation]]. Visually, it looked the same as a picket line to incoming forces but had one crucial difference; one [[Fighter wing/Legends|wing]] of ships would be in advance of the other, allowing the second wing to jump the TIE starfighters while they were engaged with the first wing. The TIEs assaulted the apparent picket line high and hard, striking the first group of Alliance fighters. When the first line began taking evasive action, the second Alliance fighter group struck. The Imperial starfighters, caught in a trap, were systemically destroyed by the older Rebel craft. As Flarestream led her fighters around for a second pass, she watched as the TIEs' [[Unidentified Imperial cruiser (Refnar)|home cruiser]] lurched into action. Usually, during similar engagements, the line cruiser stayed out of range, allowing its starfighter complement to engage the enemy. However, the [[Unidentified Imperial cruiser commander|commander]] of the cruiser was caught off-guard by Flarestream's actions and threw his ship into the battle. Several transports were destroyed by the cruiser before they could retreat into hyperspace.<ref name="CRO" />

Despite the cruiser's attack, Flarestream's tactics saved over 70 percent of the fleeing transports and nearly all the starfighters. However, Flarestream was broken by the battle, losing one too many of her friends to enemy fire. The image of the cruiser casually destroying the transports stayed in her mind; the Empire, she realized, had more firepower than it needed to destroy the Alliance, and she began to believe that there was nothing she could do but fight a losing battle. With that in mind, she decided to hang up her [[Flight suit/Legends|flight suit]] and retire from active combat duty, requesting a transfer to a posting that she considered to be "less heroic."<ref name="CRO" />

Although removed from combat, Flarestream was assigned to [[Sleeper Cell V-16]], based in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Fabrillan]], [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of the planet [[Malthor]]<ref name="CRO" /> in the [[Tandon sector/Legends|Tandon sector]] of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="TEA-OC" /> Flarestream was one of the five core members of the cell—which included [[Berren Sid Té]], a street [[Merchant/Legends|vendor]] and [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]]; [[Bakku]], a [[Reigat]] enforcer; [[Reginna Bel Typolla]], an information gatherer; and the [[Spek-Man]], a [[Droid/Legends|droid]] expert—who would occasionally meet to discuss missions and operations that focused on acquiring information and assisting other Alliance operations on the planet. Flarestream, haunted by the guilt she felt over her actions at Refnar, became an [[Alcohol/Legends|alcoholic]] and frequented Fabrillian's [[Cantina/Legends|bars]] as she waited for her orders. Only sober when on a mission, Flarestream descended into a drunken stupor as soon as her tasks were completed. She knew many pilots who were based in Fabrillian and used them to accomplish Sleeper Cell V-16's goals when necessary. When Alliance [[operative]]s not based on Malthor conducted operations on-planet, Flarestream was usually the one to extract them, using her personal [[Ghtroc Industries/Legends|Ghtroc Industries]] freighter, the ''[[Pepper's Hope]]''.<ref name="CRO" />

==Personality and traits==
Margo Flarestream was considered to be mature for her age when she was younger, and some of her Alliance contemporaries considered her to be a "visionary" for defecting from the Empire before the practice became more commonplace. An able pilot, Flarestream had experience flying starfighters, [[Realspace/Legends|space]] transports, and older-model starships, and was able to operate ground vehicles and [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] craft. She was also trained as a [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]], with expertise in vehicle [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] and starship and [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]] gunnery. Flarestream also had to the ability to [[Astrogation/Legends|plot courses]] through both space and hyperspace, and could operate [[Comm unit/Legends|communications equipment]] and [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]]. She was knowledgeable in the operation of [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] aboard starships and could repair space transports if the need arose. Trained to use a blaster, Flarestream always carried a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|pistol]] with her, along with a [[Knife/Legends|knife]]. She was trained in survival and [[Medic/Legends|first aid]], and had an interest in other cultures, [[Language/Legends|languages]] and [[Star system/Legends|planetary systems]]. She typically wore an unmarked flight suit and [[Blast vest/Legends|blast vest]].<ref name="CRO" />

At one time a confident and capable pilot, Flarestream had also shown a talent for command, although her only experience in that regard came during the battle that would see her remove herself from combat. Flarestream's feelings of guilt over the deaths of many Alliance [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] during the battle at Refnar overwhelmed her, turning her into an alcoholic. As a result, she always carried a [[Liter/Legends|liter]] [[Flask/Legends|flask]] about her person. [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]], head of [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]], was aware of Flarestream's mental state and was saddened that a capable pilot like her had had to remove herself from front line duty. He was heartened that she was still contributing to the Alliance with her work in Sleeper Cell V-16, and harbored the hope that one day she would be fit to return to combat duty.<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
Margo Flarestream was mentioned in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a supplement for the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' that was published by [[West End Games]] in [[1994]]. Her only visual depiction was illustrated by [[Mike Vilardi]].
